title: podocarpus novae-caledoniae
intro: Podocarpus novae-caledoniae, also known as the Kauri tree or Cabbage tree, is a species of evergreen conifer native to the island of New Caledonia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Podocarpus novae-caledoniae include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its usage should be carefully researched and regulated due to potential toxicity.
